supplies.title: Mungyo Soft Pastels
supplies.rationale:
- Art Instruction: for pastel drawing, painting, and color theory lessons. 
- Art History: pieces of brown and other colors will be crushed to make pigment.
supplies.note: Check local arts and crafts stores for sales and coupons.
supplies.maySub: any 24 count or more pastel set. Mungyo brand is a great, inexpensive option for students.
supplies.qty: Can be shared by 2 or 3 students
supplies.usedInText:

supplies.title: Tacky Glue
supplies.maySub: any kind of tacky glue for Sloyd or white glue for Art History.

supplies.title: Air Dry Modeling Clay
supplies.qty:
- Art Lessons: 1 per 6 students
- Art History: 1 per 4 students

supplies.title: Multi Piece Pottery Tool Kit
supplies.note: Used for shaping clay as you're modeling.
supplies.maySub: pencils, dowel rods, rolling pins, wire, plastic knives, etc.

supplies.title: Colored Masking Tape
supplies.note: Used to make a tape collage.

supplies.title: Duct Tape
supplies.note: Used for armature for paper mache project.
supplies.maySub: any duct tape or other strong tape.

supplies.title: Gold Leaf Sheets
supplies.note: Used in several Art History projects.
supplies.qty: Can be shared between multiple students

supplies.title: Sumi Ink
supplies.rationale: Sumi ink is a specific ink used for sumi-e painting and cannot be subbed with other types of ink.
supplies.maySub: with another brand of sumi ink.
supplies.qty: Can be shared by up to 6 students

supplies.title: Block Ink
supplies.rationale: Block ink is a specific ink used for print making and cannot be subbed with other types of ink. You may sub with another brand of block ink if desired.

supplies.title: Brayer
supplies.note: Used for rolling out ink and inking printing plates.

supplies.title: Gallery Glass Black Leading and Paint
supplies.note: Used in an Art History project.
supplies.maySub: any black leading for stained glass and stained glass paint.

supplies.title: Bamboo Brush
supplies.rationale: These brushes are used for a Sumi-e painting project in Term 1. They are highly recommended, but you may use round water color brushes from Art Instruction lessons as a substitute.

supplies.title: X-Acto Knife
supplies.note: Used for cutting paper and cardboard.
supplies.maySub: any style X-Acto knife or box cutter.

supplies.title: Lino Cutting Tools
supplies.note: Used for cutting design into linoleum blocks to make prints.
supplies.maySub: any other lino cutting tool. The blades can be shared if you have more handles.

supplies.title: Waterproof Metallic Pens
supplies.note: Used in a couple of Brush Drawing lessons and Art History lessons.

supplies.title: Household Items - Art History
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:	
- rock (to crush charcoal) (T1)
- disposable plates, bowls or cups (to mix pigments on/in and to hold flour/water mixture) (T1)
- sticks (T1)
- concrete outside (in some form to work on) (T1)
- different types and colors of paper (can be scraps) (T1)
- construction paper including a 12x18 piece of black construction paper (T1/3)
- color pencils, crayons, markers or tempera sticks (any of these will do for adding color) (T1/2/3)
- gallon sized ziplock bags (at least 2 per student) (T1)
- brown paper bag or brown craft paper (T1)
- flour (T1)
- balloon (8-12") (T1)
- newspaper, a lot for paper mache (T1)
- cardstock or cardboard (you can also recycle round oatmeal containers, toilet paper tubes, cereal boxes, etc.) (T1)
- terracotta colored paint (can be created by mixing brown or blue with orange) (T1)
- black and white paint (T1)
- sponge (T1)
- paper towels (T1/2)
- cardboard, sturdy piece for base of clay project (T1/2)
- loose white paper (i.e. drawing paper, rice paper, copy paper, newsprint) (T1)
- styrofoam or porcelain plate for India ink pallette (T1)
- styrofoam or glass sheet (can use styrofoam plate or sheet of glass or plexiglass from old picture frame) (T1)
- watercolors and palette (T1/3)
-  8x8" square of multimedia paper (cut this out of your art book) (T2)
- circle templates (optional) (T2)
- ball point pen (T2)
- stylus or awl/needle tool (T2)
- plastic spoon (optional) (T2/3)
- egg (T2)
- old muffin tin, plastic egg carton or paint palette for mixing colors in (T2)
- Art book or loose mixed media paper (T1/2/3)
- old bowl or plate for grinding pastels (T2)
- two sheets of cardboard measuring 11x14, can be recycled from boxes or packaging (T2)
- glass jar or glass sheet like from 8x10 picture frame (T2/3)
- cloth (T1/2/3)
